output.summary: Right-align social media icons on desktop and remove row gap between wrapped lines

fileChanges.description: Update <ul> classes to right-align items on desktop and remove row gap
fileChanges.details: On the <ul> element (line 76), change classes from 'flex flex-col gap-4 md:flex-row md:flex-wrap md:items-center' to 'flex flex-col gap-4 md:flex-row md:flex-wrap md:items-center md:justify-end md:gap-0'. The gap-4 remains for mobile (flex-col stacking). On desktop, md:gap-0 removes all gap since each <li> has its own padding. md:justify-end right-aligns items, and wrapped lines inherit the same justification.

fileChanges.description: Mirror the same <ul> class changes in PHP frontend template
fileChanges.details: On the <ul> element (line 31), change classes from 'flex flex-col gap-4 md:flex-row md:flex-wrap md:items-center' to 'flex flex-col gap-4 md:flex-row md:flex-wrap md:items-center md:justify-end md:gap-0'. Must stay in sync with TSX.

designDecisions.decision: Add md:justify-end to right-align social icons on desktop. justify-end on a wrapping flex container ensures all lines (including wrapped) are right-aligned.
designDecisions.evidence: User requested: 'Align the social media icons to the right on desktop (justify-end on the ul). When icons wrap to multiple lines, wrapped lines should also be right-aligned.'

designDecisions.decision: Add md:gap-0 to remove gap between items on desktop. Mobile retains gap-4 for vertical stacking. Item padding provides sufficient spacing on desktop.
designDecisions.evidence: User requested: 'Remove the gap between wrapped lines - the padding on each item element is sufficient spacing.'
